sn45.27:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, just as a pot without a stand is easily knocked over, while one with a stand is difficult to knock over, so the mind without a stand is easily knocked over, while the mind with a stand is difficult to knock over.
sn45.27:1.2: "And what, bhikkhus, is the stand of the mind? It is this Noble Eightfold Path; that is, right view … right concentration. This is the stand of the mind.
sn45.27:1.3: "Bhikkhus, just as a pot … so the mind without a stand is easily knocked over, while the mind with a stand is difficult to knock over."
